issues_found: H3 downstream correlation confounded by width and L0. All 5 high-absorption SAEs in C3B matched comparison are 1M-width (L0 16-58); all 5 low-absorption SAEs are 16k/65k (L0 137-297). Partial correlations control for log(width), layer, arch_class but NOT L0 (which varies 15x, range 9-445). SCR partial r inflation from -0.431 to -0.677 is a suppressor effect red flag. Without within-width-tier correlations and L0 as covariate, the H3 result may be an ecological fallacy driven by width-quality confound.

Type II taxonomy rate (88.5%, 23/26 letters) is driven by systematic measurement artifact: n_comparison_tokens=0 for almost all letters forces fallback to global mean-when-active baseline biased upward. The resulting 92.3% comprehensive rate is presented in abstract, introduction, and conclusion as a primary finding, contradicting the paper's own CRITICAL suspicious_flag acknowledging inflation. Only Type I (3.8%) and Type III (0%) are validated; 92.3% is an upper bound, not a measurement.

LV detector pre-filter (decoder cosine > 0.15) captures only 34% of absorbed pairs, imposing a fundamental recall ceiling of 0.34. The F1 > 0.35 success criterion was structurally unachievable before alpha_ij was evaluated. The paper reports F1=0.128 but does not disambiguate whether failure is due to pre-filter coverage or alpha_ij discrimination ability within the filtered set. ROC-AUC=0.148 (below 0.5 = random) indicates anti-prediction, which is more informative than F1 but is not prominently reported.

Safety probe experiment (C3C, Table 6) is underpowered (n=3 SAEs) and confounded (layers 5/8/10, widths 768/24k/32k, different hook types). Non-monotone pattern (highest absorption = smallest probe gap: 0.051) directly undermines the H3 narrative at the single-experiment level. At n=3 with 3+ confounding factors, the experiment contributes noise, not signal. Presenting it alongside the strong SAEBench results in Section 5.4 creates a false equivalence.

Cross-model gap between GPT-2 Small (H1/H2/H4/taxonomy) and Gemma Scope (H3) means no single hypothesis is tested on both models. The paper frames itself as a 'unified three-part study' in abstract and conclusion, but it is actually two disconnected analyses. A reviewer will ask: does the LV coefficient also fail on Gemma? Does the taxonomy hold on Gemma? Does the PMI null hold on Gemma? Without same-model replication, the results cannot be composed into a single narrative.

PMI regression (C2C) uses HC3 robust standard errors but does not cluster at the letter level. The 806 observations are 31 configs x 26 letters; each letter appears 31 times with correlated residuals. L0 significance (p=0.012) is marginal and likely non-significant under proper letter-level clustering (26 clusters). The Durbin-Watson statistic of 1.335 indicates positive autocorrelation; Jarque-Bera p=0.00 confirms non-normality; skewness=5.186 suggests a zero-inflated or beta distribution would be more appropriate than OLS.

H4 (width paradox) DAS results show high per-letter variance (e.g., letter N: DAS(k=1) = 0.571 at 24k but 0.100 at 98k; letter X: 0.0 to 1.0 at 98k) with no confidence intervals on mean DAS values and no significance tests comparing means across widths. Only 42.3% of letters show positive slope (target 80%). 'Partial support' framing is not warranted without formal tests. DAS estimation uses n_word_act_samples=40 per letter, not the 10,000 tokens claimed in methodology.

Abstract uses 'validated quality indicator' to describe H3 downstream correlation. Correlational evidence cannot 'validate' a metric; it shows association. 'Validated' implies interventional evidence (reduce absorption, observe quality improvement) that does not exist. The conclusion repeats this overclaim.

Title 'When Features Compete' implies the competitive exclusion framework is supported. The paper's main finding is that competition-based detection fails (F1=0.128, ROC-AUC=0.148). The title creates a misleading first impression.

Figure 1 (LV framework conceptual diagram, fig_lv_framework.pdf) does not exist as a rendered PDF. Only fig_lv_framework_desc.md exists in writing/figures/. The paper references 'Figure 1' in Introduction and Section 3. Without this figure, the paper cannot be compiled for external review. Four other figures (fig_das_width.pdf, fig_downstream_scatter.pdf, fig_sharpness.pdf, fig_taxonomy_bar.pdf) exist and are well-generated.
issues_fixed: RECURRING BLOCKER RESOLVED: All core experiments now run at FULL scale (C1B: 1,900 pairs; C2B: 806 observations across 31 SAEs; C3A: 54 Gemma SAEs; C1D: 26 letters at 3 widths; C2D: 26 letters full taxonomy). After 3 consecutive iterations stuck at PILOT mode, the pilot-to-full escalation was successfully executed in iter_004.

Complete research pivot from failed OMP/EncNorm approach to LV competitive exclusion framework with clean hypothesis structure (H1-H4 with pre-registered success criteria). This resolved the stagnation at 5.5 for 3 iterations.

Cross-model validation achieved for H3: Gemma Scope 54 SAEs used for downstream correlation, GPT-2 used for detection/taxonomy. Gemma access issue resolved (not via HuggingFace gating but via SAEBench pre-computed data).

All 4 required figures generated (fig_das_width.pdf, fig_downstream_scatter.pdf, fig_sharpness.pdf, fig_taxonomy_bar.pdf). Previous 3 iterations had zero or stale figures. Fig 1 (LV framework diagram) still missing.

Negative result reporting maintained and expanded: H1 (F1=0.128), H2 (partial R^2=0.0006), H4 (42.3% vs 80% target) all reported with pre-registered criteria. This remains the paper's strongest methodological aspect.
Writing quality substantially improved: clear structure, honest hedging, appropriate statistical reporting. Writing review score 8/10, up from previous iterations.
OMP experiment ceiling issue resolved: the entire OMP approach was abandoned in favor of the LV framework, eliminating the pilot-scale ceiling problem.
Label set circularity resolved: the paper uses sae-spelling ground truth for H1/H2 and SAEBench absorption scores for H3, rather than re-using the same n_pos=18 label set from iter_001.
TopK-32k AUROC misrepresentation resolved: the paper no longer presents proxy-label AUROC as cross-architecture absorption detection replication.
Section 4.1/3.4 redundancy resolved: the paper has a clean Section 3/4 structure without verbatim duplication.
